Fororo

Inhabitant of Yovera
Vocation: Exalted Monk
Level: 518

Posts: 1

 
Monk Spirit Mend(SOLO HEALING) and Bossing



There are certain inherent weaknesses regarding the Monk vocation that cannot be overlooked:

1. Survivability and Scaling: It is meaningless that a high level let's say 600+ Monk heals for a mere 500–800 with ours most powerful healing spell. Spirit Mend urgently requires a significant potency increase; it currently feels identical to a Knight's outdated Exura Med Ico. Paradoxically, while the Knight's healing mechanics were reworked to eliminate repetitive spamming, the Monk remains restricted to the exact archaic mechanic developers intended to phase out about exura med ico spamming.

2. Boss Viability and Endgame Exclusion: As frequently highlighted by the community, the Monk currently serves as a liability during boss encounters. It is imperative to design an effective, high-yield ranged rotation for this vocation; otherwise, Monks will remain permanently excluded from late-game boss content.

3. Elemental Constraints: Restricted strictly to Physical, Energy, and Earth damage, the Monk faces severe limitations when selecting viable hunting grounds. In contrast, all other vocations possess comprehensive tools covering the entire elemental spectrum.

Additionally, the Monk’s virtues must be thoroughly reviewed. Following a succession of nerfs, none of them feel genuinely rewarding or possess a well-defined identity. Should consider reducing the selection to just two distinct virtues by merging the remaining ones. A clear example of this successful design is the Paladin, which has two well-defined stances: one tailored for enhanced tanking and the other focused on offensive output. In contrast, the Monk's current virtues feel fragmented, with none proving to be sufficiently useful in late-game scenarios.

Please do not let this vocation become obsolete. It is vital to address the Monk’s survivability in both solo and team-hunting scenarios. Spirit Mend currently feels like a basic Exura Gran in the late game, which is simply unviable for high-level content.

Post #39586787
Quote
 





Immortal Drigo
Creature of Habit (Grade 1)


Inhabitant of Antica
Vocation: Druid
Level: 302

Posts: 122

 
This game feature rewards Power Abusers and Griefers only.



Name me at least one not griefing downside of free PvE logout and why ppl should get banned for that or why that is considered game abusement in your eyes?

I think Tibia is the only game in the world with such outdated bias that impacts players in such a negative way.

When you log out at bosses you get tped out of the boss room and you lose your cooldown, it's your loss.

During PvP you can't log out.

How it's hurting your griefing mind that players could log out during PvE hunt anytime they want? I don't really get it, Tibia is the only game left with such outdated mechanics which only benefits power abusers.

I just don't get the reason why this is still a thing in 2026? Because you're such a grief and you want people to keep dying unfairly and lose everything they've been working on for hours or days just because of weak and unfair internet connection loss so they would rage quit the game?

If that is the thing it means that you're a griefer.

Ask yourself a question how many players you know yourself that have quit the game because they don't own a good internet connection and they don't want to lose time just because of this?


There are no any real downsides to it anyways beside bringing back thousands of retired people with weaker internet connection back to this game...

Griefing and abusement should stop being a thing in 2026 because that is what ruins this game in reality.

Only power abusers on dominando servers would vote for that to continue I imagine because they wouldn't be able to blackmail their victims anymore when they could log out on their hunting spots.

Tibia got it all the way opposite currently, it's griefers being rewarded and victims are being punished, especially on dominando servers or because someone has worse internet connection which happens a lot and no you can't upgrade your internet connection in all locations that's why so many people left this game, they don't want to lose hours or days of work in a minute just because of unfair internet connection loss, that's why they quit when they die and no they are not selling their characters - that happens but seldom.

CipSoft would greatly benefit from fixing this game feature which currently rewards power abusers only.

I can clearly understand that players that are engaged in PvP in any way shouldn't be able to log out but PvE? Come on guys really, it's 2026 game... It should at least meet basic standards like this and like I've mentioned it's griefers and power abusers who should rather focus on their own gameplay. Only griefers care about other players logging out to avoid deaths, unbox themselves or for whatever other reason, who else cares and does that on purpose in the first place anyway? Why would I even care about that while I'm not a griefer?

Probably there are unboxing spells that are coming in the future so argument about lack of skill will not be valid at all and what then? Isn't it better to fix the game for everyone with that simple fix rather than creating some weird unboxing spells for minority only?

When you lose internet connection it has nothing to do with the lack of a skill. That's the main problem...

That was the only problem that me and my friends had and are having with this game, that's why many have quit and it was never fixed, reality is that anyone at any point can lose their connection and die and it's just simply unfair.

If you don't want to take my word for it just create a poll where you ask players if they are afraid to lose their connection and die from PvE so you can get the clear picture.

This is very outdated game mechanic it's time to change this.

It's 2026 it's really the time...

Dragondouser


Inhabitant of Antica
Vocation: Elder Druid
Level: 857

Chaos Rider of the Black Dragons

Posts: 62




Originally posted by Last Orc on 06.06.2026 14:27:44:


Are you telling me a knight doesn't need all the equipment for every element? A knight whose main purpose is blocking damage? I think you've forgotten that knight also have six shields, at least three armors (for example, two Spirithorn armors + a falcon), and a lot of helmets. What's more, your six magical armors have a lower total cost than one Spirithorn armor, not to mention the cost of legs, which rarely cost less than 350kk, so one of our legs costs twice as much as six magical ones. Still going to debate this?
That's the same as saying druids should have 10 soulshroud because its the best robe in game for eds...one for each imbuement xD! ofc course we can also go on 7-10 elven mails, which we do...

You can always change the element by using a imbuement scroll on the go nowadays, no biggie  keep using your t3 spiritthorn armor and change the imbue, on the long run this is 100000% cheaper than tiering 5 of them to T3, unless my math is wrong.

Also, last time i checked shields didn't get Tiers. What about them?

Post #39586808

Hayaku Kazehaya
Truly Boss


Inhabitant of Secura
Vocation: Master Sorcerer
Level: 592

Leader of the Elite Brotherhood

Posts: 80




Originally posted by Maiar Ungoliant on 07.06.2026 01:25:35:

You are dishonest - one simple youtube search and I've found a lvl 800 MS doing 9.1kk raw on dragolisk WITHOUT CHARMS during the test server:
https://youtu.be/RYWUZGHhuYc?si=abMlkENTsjw_43Xb

So to your point, a MS with proper CHARMS and gems would do WAY MORE on nimmersatt dragons than an ED - a spot sensitive to both earth and ice would be best enjoyed by MS.
Regarding teamhunts - I agree that ED damage could be lower on parties, but they should have the option to do damage solo.
I repeat - MS is fine. ED will only do great solo on Ice sensitive respawns, which is VERY limiting.
MS will do well on energy and death sensitive respawns, and will be viable on fire sensitive respawns although Fire wave still needs some love from CIP.
STOP BEING CONCERNED BY EDs - MS got great changes, fork spells are still underwhelming high level (maybe low level they are good, but their base damage is nothing crazy and its limited to 7 creatures)
I'm not gonna talk about EDs because that's not my voc but MS both here and in the infamous solo 17kk raw exp video is heavily lifted by the OP Beam Mastery. Beam Mastery should focus on making beams stronger but it also reduces cooldowns and that is what creates those beastly raw gains combined with elemental stances. Once you take away Beam Mastery, MS is not going to be meaningfully more powerful than other vocs. It's kinda like ED is currently weak without Ulus. Beam Mastery needs a nerf while for instance Great Fire Wave perks need a buff.
